description: Rules proposed automatically by the Phase LEARN pipeline based on trending rubric-criterion failures. Each rule has a human approval gate before it ships.

guidelines.rule: Only use card components when they serve to group distinct, complex information or provide clear interactive elements, avoiding their use for simple lists or homogeneous data that could be better presented otherwise.
guidelines.why: This rule addresses an 83% failure rate on the CARDS_JUSTIFIED criterion across 6 recent evaluations, where cards were frequently used without clear justification, leading to cluttered or inefficient UIs.
examples: BAD: A list of simple text items where each item is unnecessarily wrapped in a card, adding visual clutter without functional benefit.
GOOD: A collection of distinct entities (e.g., products, team members) where each card groups multiple data points, an image, and specific actions.
